commit.message:
fix(hot-reload): version-aware assembly resolution, CompilationContext pipeline, lazy Harmony patching

- resolveAssembly now compares assembly versions, skipping lower versions
  to prevent 0x80131040 manifest mismatch errors
- File watcher uses CompilationContext pipeline (parseFileStructureCached +
  preprocessForFsi) instead of #load, avoiding re-execution of module-level
  side effects like server startup
- Falls back to #load only if CompilationContext parse fails
- Per-watcher CompilationState tracks evaluated modules across reloads
- isWatched false branch now logs diagnostic (likely root cause of demo failure)
- DevReloadInjector uses Interlocked.CompareExchange for thread-safe one-shot
  guard, resets on failure for retry, logs exceptions in tryPatch
- 8 new tests: 3 version-aware resolution + 5 CompilationContext integration
- Assembly search path tests wrapped in testSequenced to prevent shared state races

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Merge pull request #126 from dchu3/feature/paid-analysis-server

feat: add paid token analysis MCP server with x402 USDC payments

Add a paid analysis service that exposes the existing Gemini-powered
TokenAnalyzer pipeline as an MCP tool behind an x402 USDC paywall
on Solana.

New components:
- app/api_server.py: FastAPI endpoint wrapping TokenAnalyzer
- app/cli.py: --http-api flag to run the FastAPI server
- server/: TypeScript MCP server (payment gateway)
  - src/index.ts: McpServer with analyze_token tool + x402 enforcement
  - src/payments.ts: Payment requirements from env vars
  - Dockerfile: Node 22 Alpine production image
- docker-compose.yml: api-service + analysis-server containers
- .env.example: SERVER_WALLET_ADDRESS, SERVER_PRICE_ANALYZE, etc.

The Python API is internal-only (Docker network); the TypeScript
server is the public entry point on port 4022. Existing bot
functionality is completely untouched.

commit.message:
Fix E2E CI: Docker Hub rate limits + v2.x backfill priority + idempotent builds (#392)

* Initial plan

* Fix Docker Hub v2.x image push issues in CI workflows

- backfill-docker.yml: change sort -V to sort -rV so v2.x versions
  are processed first when max_versions limit is applied, preventing
  recent releases from being starved by old v1.x backlog
- backfill-docker.yml: add idempotency check before builds to skip
  versions already fully pushed to Docker Hub
- release.yml: add idempotency check in build job to skip rebuild
  when the version+arch already exists on Docker Hub, using existing
  digest for artifact upload so merge job still creates the manifest

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix E2E CI: add cache-to and release cache to Docker build step

The Build test image step had cache-from but no cache-to, so the GHA
build cache was never written. Each E2E run pulled node:25-alpine and
python:3.14-slim from Docker Hub fresh, hitting 429 rate-limit errors.

Fixes:
- Add cache-to: type=gha,mode=max,scope=build-test so layers are
  saved and reused on subsequent runs
- Add cache-from: type=gha,scope=build-linux/amd64 (the release build
  cache written on every main branch merge) so base images are already
  cached from the very first run on a new PR

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix COM ref counting in SOSMethodEnum simulator and bump SOS_BREAKING_CHANGE_VERSION (#5746)

## Summary

Matching PR for dotnet/runtime#125231, which fixes
`DefaultCOMImpl::Release()` reference counting bugs in the DAC.

### Changes

**1. Bump `SOS_BREAKING_CHANGE_VERSION` from 5 to 6** (`sospriv.h`,
`sospriv.idl`)

The runtime PR bumps this version. Without updating SOS's copy, users
would see a spurious "SOS needs to be upgraded" warning when debugging
newer runtimes.

**2. Fix `SOSMethodEnum` simulator COM ref counting** (`util.cpp`)

The `SOSDacInterface15` simulator's `GetMethodTableSlotEnumerator` had
the same bug pattern fixed in the runtime PR — raw pointer assignment
without `QueryInterface`, and the null check occurred after the
dereference. Fixed to:
- Initialize `refCount` to 0 (matching `DefaultCOMImpl` convention)
- Use `QueryInterface` to return the interface, properly calling
`AddRef`
- Check for null before dereferencing the out parameter
- Use `delete` on failure (since `QueryInterface` wasn't called at
refCount 0)

**3. Fix `ISOSMethodEnum` leak in `strike.cpp`**

Changed raw `ISOSMethodEnum*` to `ToRelease<ISOSMethodEnum>` so
`Release()` is called when the pointer goes out of scope, matching the
pattern used everywhere else (e.g., `ToRelease<ISOSHandleEnum>`).

Co-authored-by: Max Charlamb <maxcharlamb@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(kernels): AVX2+FMA log-softmax with vectorized exp and SIMD reduce (#3360)

## Summary

Add AVX2 fast path to \log_softmax_f32\ the log-softmax kernel that was
purely scalar.

### Changes

**Implementation** (\softmax.rs\):
- \log_softmax_avx2()\  three-pass AVX2 log-softmax:
  - Pass 1: 8-wide SIMD max reduction
- Pass 2: Vectorized \xp_avx2()\ sum accumulation (same Cephes
polynomial as softmax)
  - Pass 3: SIMD subtraction (\input[i] - log_sum_exp\)
- Runtime dispatch in \log_softmax_f32()\: AVX2+FMA detected fast path,
else scalar fallback
- Tail handling for non-8-aligned lengths

**Tests** (\softmax.rs\ inline + \vx2_scalar_parity_tests.rs\
external):
- \ est_log_softmax_avx2_sized\ 32-element vector exercises 4 full AVX2
chunks
- \ est_log_softmax_matches_log_of_softmax\ 64-element \log(softmax(x))
log_softmax(x)\ parity
- \log_softmax_parity_aligned\ aligned sizes (8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256)
vs reference scalar
- \log_softmax_parity_unaligned\ unaligned sizes (1, 3, 7, 9, 15, 17,
31, 33, 65) vs reference
- \log_softmax_stability_large_values\ numerical stability with inputs
~1000

### Test Results

All 21 log_softmax tests pass (16 existing + 5 new).

### Receipt
- **What changed**: AVX2 dispatch added to \log_softmax_f32\; 5 new
parity tests
- **Local**: \cargo test -p bitnet-kernels --no-default-features
--features cpu -- log_softmax\ — 21/21 pass
- **Parity evidence**: 3 external parity tests in
\vx2_scalar_parity_tests.rs\ compare against f64-precision scalar
reference
- **Docs**: none (internal kernel; no public API change)
- **Risks**: none runtime dispatch ensures fallback on non-AVX2 hardware
- **Disposition**: A (MERGE NOW)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

## What does this add?

## Type of changes

Please check the type of change your PR introduces:

- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing
functionality to not work as expected)
- [x] 📚 Documentation update (improvements or corrections to
documentation)
- [ ] 🎨 Code style/formatting (changes that do not affect the meaning of
the code)
- [ ] ♻️ Refactoring (code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a
feature)
- [ ] ⚡ Performance improvement (code change that improves performance)
- [ ] ✅ Test update (adding missing tests or correcting existing tests)
- [ ] 🔧 Build/CI changes (changes to build process or continuous
integration)
- [ ] 🗑️ Chore (other changes that don't modify src or test files)

commit.message:
fix(deploy): disable SSR/prerendering for static hosting on Render

The SSR prerendering was generating an empty browser/index.html for the
root redirect route (/ -> /blog), which caused Render to serve 0-byte
responses for all routes via the dashboard rewrite rule (/* -> /index.html).

Disabling SSR and prerendering produces a simple CSR build where Angular
generates index.html directly with content. All SEO meta tags and JSON-LD
still work client-side via SeoService.

SSR can be re-enabled later with a proper Node.js server deployment.

Optimize friend lookups using FriendByPublicKey API

Replace manual friend map iteration with toxcore's built-in FriendByPublicKey
API in handleFriendTextIn and handleFriendFileIn. This eliminates the need to
hold a read lock while iterating the friends map and improves code quality.

Improvements:
- Reduced cyclomatic complexity of handleFriendTextIn from 9 to 7 (22.2%)
- Reduced cyclomatic complexity of handleFriendFileIn from 12 to 10 (16.7%)
- Reduced overall complexity scores by 18-24%
- Eliminated unnecessary locking during friend lookups

Completes PLAN.md Step 3.3: Use FriendByPublicKey for Lookups

commit.message:
feat: rewrite shoulder detection with noise-adaptive algorithm and inflection-point bounds

- Replace gradient-of-gradient with Savgol built-in derivatives (13x noise reduction)
- Noise-adaptive thresholds via MAD from baseline regions only
- Curvature-based per-peak exclusion zones (σ = √(A/|d2y|))
- First-derivative slope check filters valley false positives
- Asymmetric shoulder bounds: inner bound at d2y inflection point,
  outer bound walks to 2% of shoulder height above baseline
- Replace confusing height_factor (0.01-0.10) with sensitivity slider (1-10)
- Add collapsible Advanced section for Savgol window/polyorder
- Add shoulder_sensitivity to API model with backward compat

commit.message:
feat: real-time agent streaming, STOP/RESEND controls, hardened CI e2e test, and reliable agent file creation (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: STOP/RESEND buttons, agent streaming, enhanced CI e2e test

- Add POST /chat/stream SSE endpoint: streams agent_start, agent_message,
  done, stopped, error events via graph.astream(stream_mode='values')
- Add GET /run/{run_id}/files endpoint: lists workspace files for CI
- Update chat UI: Send disables during run, Stop button aborts SSE stream
  via AbortController, Resend button appears after user stops processing
- Agent replies rendered as green-bordered .agent bubbles in real-time
- Add _extract_last_message_content() helper (dict + object messages)
- Add _AGENT_EMOJIS / _AGENT_NAMES constants for all 6 agents
- Enhance CI e2e: expanded goal (main.py + test + README), max_iterations
  5, max-time 300s, verify files via /run/{run_id}/files, execute main.py
  inside container and assert output contains 'Hello'
- Add 37 new tests (86 total, all passing), ruff clean, mypy clean

Co-authored-by: clebersantz <5575670+clebersantz@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add tool execution loop to agents so tools (create_file etc.) are actually called

Previously every agent called `agent_llm.ainvoke(messages)`, received an
AIMessage that may contain tool_calls, then silently discarded those calls —
reading only `response.content`. This meant create_file, run_command and all
other tools were never invoked, leaving the workspace empty and causing the
CI e2e file-existence / program-execution checks to fail.

Fix:
- Add `src/agents/_tool_executor.py` with `run_tool_loop()`: repeatedly invokes
  the LLM, executes every tool call it returns (via `tool.ainvoke()`), feeds
  results back as ToolMessages, and stops when the LLM produces a response with
  no further tool calls (or after 10 rounds).
- Apply `run_tool_loop` in developer, documentation, qa, architect, and security
  agents (orchestrator left unchanged: GitHub tools fail with placeholder token
  and its routing logic only needs response.content keywords).
- Add 3 new tests in TestToolExecutionLoop:
  - developer executes tool calls (ainvoke called twice)
  - documentation executes tool calls
  - tool errors are fed back as ToolMessages and don't crash the agent
- All 89 tests pass; ruff clean; mypy clean; CodeQL 0 alerts.

Co-authored-by: clebersantz <5575670+clebersantz@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: ruff format tests, strengthen agent prompts to enforce tool use with absolute workspace paths

Lint failure: ruff format --check found tests/test_agents.py needed reformatting.
Applied `ruff format` to fix it.

Empty workspace root cause (still present after run_tool_loop): the LLM (Azure
OpenAI) was producing plain-text responses without tool_calls because the agent
prompts only described high-level steps and never explicitly required tool use.

Developer, documentation, QA, and architect agent prompts now:
- Add a bold IMPORTANT block: "You MUST use the available tools to actually
  create/run files. Do NOT just describe — call the tools."
- Provide concrete examples of absolute paths using {state.workspace_path}
- Tell the developer to skip git/clone/PR steps unless a repo URL is provided
  (the CI goal says "Do not use git or GitHub")
- Tell QA to use list_directory + create_file + run_command explicitly

All 89 tests pass; ruff clean; mypy clean; CodeQL 0 alerts.

Co-authored-by: clebersantz <5575670+clebersantz@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove tool binding from orchestrator to prevent empty routing responses

Root cause of persistent empty workspace:

The orchestrator called `llm.bind_tools(github_tools)` and `agent_llm.ainvoke(messages)`.
Azure OpenAI, when tools are bound, chooses to emit tool_calls (e.g. create_issue)
instead of plain text. When it does this, `response.content = ""`.
`_parse_next_agent("")` returns None, so `next_agent = None` and the graph
routes to END every single iteration — developer/qa/documentation agents were
NEVER called. After 5 iterations the workflow ended as "completed" with an
empty workspace.

Fix: Remove `bind_tools` from the orchestrator. The orchestrator only needs
to emit routing text (architect/developer/qa/security/documentation/DONE).
Using the raw `llm.ainvoke` guarantees a text response that `_parse_next_agent`
can parse correctly.

Also update `mock_llm` fixture: add `llm.ainvoke = AsyncMock(...)` so orchestrator
tests work without bind_tools, while keeping `llm.bind_tools.return_value = bound_llm`
for all other agent tests.

All 89 tests pass; ruff+mypy clean; CodeQL 0 alerts.

Co-authored-by: clebersantz <5575670+clebersantz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update CI workflow to trigger only on main branch

* fix: fallback file extraction, mock LLM e2e, improved orchestrator routing

Four layered fixes to finally eliminate the empty-workspace CI failure:

1. **Fallback file extraction** (`_tool_executor.py`):
   Added `extract_files_from_content()` and `_workspace_is_empty()`. When the
   LLM returns a text response instead of tool_calls, the agents now parse
   two output formats from the response:
   - JSON code block: ```json\n{"files": [{"path": ..., "content": ...}]}\n```
   - Markdown bold-filename header: **filename.ext**\n```lang\ncode\n```

2. **Agent fallback usage** (`developer.py`, `documentation.py`, `qa.py`):
   Each agent calls `extract_files_from_content` after `run_tool_loop` if the
   workspace is still empty, with a `isinstance(..., str)` type-safe cast.
   Prompts now explicitly request the JSON fallback format so the LLM knows
   what shape to use when it cannot call tools.

3. **Improved `_parse_next_agent`** (`orchestrator.py`):
   Rewritten to check JSON `"next_agent"` field first (priority over keyword
   scan) and check done/complete BEFORE agent names — preventing the bug where
   "The developer finished; project is DONE" would route back to developer.
   Also moved `json`/`re` imports to module level.  Orchestrator system prompt
   updated to request JSON format and no longer mentions GitHub tools.

4. **Mock LLM provider** (`graph.py`, `config.py`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`):
   Added `LLM_PROVIDER=mock` that returns a pre-canned JSON file bundle so the
   full agent pipeline (routing + file creation + workspace check + execution)
   can be tested without real credentials. CI now runs a mandatory mock e2e
   step (always passes regardless of secrets) plus the existing real-LLM step
   (skipped when secrets absent).

Local Docker e2e test with mock LLM verified:
- /run returns status=completed in 2 iterations
- workspace contains main.py, test_main.py, README.md
- `python3 main.py` prints "Hello, World!"

89 tests pass; ruff + mypy + CodeQL all clean.

Co-authored-by: clebersantz <5575670+clebersantz@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove mock LLM e2e step — CI uses real LLM_PROVIDER from repository variable

Co-authored-by: clebersantz <5575670+clebersantz@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: clebersantz <5575670+clebersantz@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
consensus: VRF eligibility uses 3-block rolling participation window

Replace 'all active masternodes' VRF pool with a rolling 3-block window.
A node is eligible for VRF sortition if it appeared in the bitmap (or
was the block producer) in any of the last 3 blocks.

This preserves the participation requirement (new nodes that haven't voted
yet are excluded) while ensuring high-latency nodes whose vote arrived
slightly late for one round are still covered by the prior round's bitmap.
A node must miss 3 consecutive rounds before losing VRF eligibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use dynamic IterationInterval() for Tox main loop

Replace hardcoded 50ms ticker with toxcore's dynamic IterationInterval()
method for optimal performance. The interval adapts to current network
conditions and protocol state automatically.

Changes:
- Replaced time.NewTicker with default case + time.Sleep pattern
- Uses c.tox.IterationInterval() to get recommended interval
- Maintains responsive shutdown via select statement
- No change in shutdown behavior or race conditions

Tests: All pass with race detection
Complexity: No regression (Quality Score: 100.0/100)

Completes: Step 3.2 from PLAN.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: harden pip install against supply chain attacks (#686)

Resolves code scanning alert #94 (pip install without hash verification).

- Expand requirements.txt via pip-compile to pin all transitive dependencies
  to exact versions (5 top-level → 17 total packages)
- Add --no-deps to Dockerfile pip install to prevent pip from resolving
  any packages beyond what is explicitly listed

This follows the approach recommended in the Opengrep rule guidance:
'use pip install --no-deps -r requirements.txt when using pip-compile
workflow.' With all transitive deps pinned and --no-deps preventing
runtime dependency resolution, no unvetted packages can be introduced.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Webview tests (75 tests):

- characters: creation, state machine (idle→walk→type), seat placement
- officeState: add/remove agents, seat assignment, layout rebuild
- layoutManager: tile maps, seat creation, furniture instances
- editorState: tool/tile/furniture selection, colors, reset
